rationale: Build on the current adaptive row-offset quantizer without spending more artifact bytes: during the late quant-aware phase, keep int8-bound matrices conservative while allowing the matrices already preserved in FP16 in the final artifact to keep adapting faster. That gives the late decoder/tail rescue path more capacity to absorb upstream int8 error in the exact final regime, while leaving the hot training loop and final evaluation path otherwise unchanged. I also removed a few lines of incidental verbosity so the script stays under the 1500-line limit.
title: Selective FP16-Tail Quant-Aware Matrix LR Split
